evidence_quotes.quote: if one does buy a game one can keep using it for as long as one wishes.

There are many examples of games that people bought, or invested money in it and then the publisher/developer out of the blue decided to shut down the game and thus leaving the buyer with no access to something that was his/hers, to name a few The Crew and Nier Reincarnation.

paragraphs.text: I think that this act is a very welcoming one for the digital media of all kinds. I do have a recommendation though, and that is to add an extra problematic practice to the list which would go hand in hand with this initiative. I am referring to Stop Destroying Videogames CI(2024)000007 which has reached over 1.4mil signatures and with that we want to make sure that if one does buy a game one can keep using it for as long as one wishes. There are many examples of games that people bought, or invested money in it and then the publisher/developer out of the blue decided to shut down the game and thus leaving the buyer with no access to something that was his/hers, to name a few The Crew and Nier Reincarnation. There is an entire list on a wiki page search for "dead videogames". I, as a gamer and now working in IT think that videogames can be seen as work of art, something that must be preserved, even more so if people bought them in the first place. Kind regards.
